reports.text: रामनवमीं की हार्दिक शुभकामनाएं..

Fertilizer prices are skyrocketing:

New Orleans granular urea prices, the most widely used nitrogen fertilizer, are up +89% since December, to $660 per short ton, the highest since September 2022.

By comparison, the 2022 peak during the Russia-Ukraine war was $910 per short ton.

This comes as the Strait of Hormuz shutdown has effectively halted fertilizer shipments from the Middle East.

Furthermore, top exporters China and Russia are also curbing crop nutrient sales, tightening supply further at the worst possible time, right before the spring planting season.

Higher fertilizer costs directly translate to higher food prices, threatening to reignite agricultural inflation as seen in 2022.

Credits: The Kobeissi Letter

reports.text:
When Oil Prices Spike, Economies Hit the Brakes

• Fuel rationing, WFH mandates, odd even rules
• Industrial slowdown, reduced mobility
• Governments stepping in to control demand

The insight

Oil acts as an economic pressure point
When prices spike, demand doesn’t adjust naturally
It gets forced down through policy

What it means

• Consumption slows
• Growth moderates
• Demand destruction kicks in

High oil prices eventually correct
Not because supply improves
But because demand breaks

reports.text:
BREAKING: Iran has officially rejected the US' peace proposal and sets five conditions for an end to the war.

Iran's conditions include:

1. Immediate end to attacks and assassinations on Iran

2. Establishment of "concrete guarantees" against future US attacks 

3. "Clear determination and guaranteed payment" for war damages

4. International recognition of Iran's "authority" over Strait of Hormuz 

5. An end to the war across all fronts, including for all Iranian proxies in the region

We now await a response from the US.

reports.text:
**OIL & GAS: CLSA ON POST-WAR OUTLOOK (HIGHER-FOR-LONGER CRUDE)**

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Ceasefire likely to trigger sharp correction in crude, LNG & tanker rates  
• Short-term relief rally expected in OMCs and gas stocks  
• Medium-term view: crude & LNG to remain structurally higher (10–20% above pre-war)  
• Supply disruptions and energy security focus to keep prices elevated  

**MARKET REACTION (POST WAR)**  
• Sharp pullback expected in crude, LNG and freight rates  
• OMCs (IOC/BPCL/HPCL) down ~24–26% → likely strong relief rally  
• Gas stocks (GAIL, GSPL, IGL, MGL, Gujarat Gas, PLNG) down ~10–18% → rebound expected  
• First phase rally driven by normalization of prices  

**STRUCTURAL INDUSTRY OUTLOOK**  
• Supply recovery delayed; potential permanent loss in some oil & LNG capacity  
• LNG oversupply pushed from 2026 → 2027 due to infrastructure damage  
• Higher demand from energy security and inventory rebuilding  
• Tightened demand-supply balance supports elevated pricing  

**OMC ECONOMICS**  
• Break-even Brent for fuels: ~$75–80/bbl  
• Crude unlikely to sustain below this level → margin pressure persists  
• Retail fuel prices unlikely to fully adjust due to policy constraints  
• Marketing margins already at record lows  

**GAS SECTOR OUTLOOK**  
• LNG prices structurally higher; limited near-term triggers  
• GAIL, PLNG, Gujarat Gas may see muted medium-term outlook  
• City gas (IGL, MGL) relatively better positioned with policy support (PNG push)  

**STOCK-LEVEL VIEW**  
• **Upstream (ONGC, OIL, RIL):** Key beneficiaries of higher crude prices  
• ONGC seen as preferred play; significant upside at elevated crude levels  
• **OMCs:** Strong short-term bounce but weak medium-term margin outlook  
• **Gas Mid/Downstream:** Relief rally likely, but limited structural triggers  

**BUSINESS TRENDS**  
• Shift towards energy security globally  
• Higher structural crude and LNG pricing environment  
• Increased volatility in energy markets  

**IMPACT**  
• Mixed – Short-term positive for OMCs and gas stocks (relief rally), but medium-term favors upstream players due to structurally higher energy prices

**INDIA STRATEGY: BERNSTEIN FLAGS GFC-LIKE RISK SCENARIO**

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Bernstein highlights potential “GFC-like” downside risk if crude shock persists  
• Nifty already down ~12% YTD amid ongoing Middle East tensions  
• Macro risks driven by external vulnerability (CAD, rupee, inflation)  
• Maintains Neutral stance on Indian equities  

**MACRO OUTLOOK**  
• Risk of GDP slowing to ~2–3% in prolonged crisis scenario  
• Inflation likely to breach RBI’s 6% tolerance band  
• Rate cuts delayed or completely priced out for CY26  
• Yield curve flattening indicates tighter financial conditions  

**EXTERNAL SECTOR RISKS**  
• Rising crude → widening trade deficit and CAD (~2.5% of GDP)  
• Weakening services surplus due to global slowdown  
• Forex reserves quality deteriorating (lower FCA share, higher gold mix)  
• Import cover falling to multi-decade lows  

**RUPEE & LIQUIDITY**  
• Rupee expected to weaken towards ₹97–98 in base case  
• Extreme scenario: USDINR >110 if crisis prolongs  
• RBI intervention capacity constrained due to lower liquid reserves  
• FII outflows and weaker remittances add pressure  

**MARKET SCENARIOS (NIFTY)**  
• Bull case: ~27,500 (quick de-escalation)  
• Base case: ~26,000 (1-month resolution)  
• Bear case: ~23,000–24,100 (prolonged conflict)  
• Crisis case: ~19,000–20,000 or lower  

**BUSINESS & MARKET TRENDS**  
• High crude impacts core inflation via second-order effects  
• Markets transitioning from growth optimism to macro stress  
• Volatility expected to remain elevated  
• Earnings and growth cycle may get delayed by 3–4 years  

**SECTORAL IMPACT**  
• Negative: Airlines, OMCs, chemicals, logistics, consumption  
• Relative beneficiaries: Select energy and export-oriented sectors  
• Crude-sensitive sectors likely to underperform  

**STRATEGIC VIEW**  
• Advises caution; wait for clear signs of de-escalation  
• Any relief rally likely to be short-lived  
• Risk-reward not attractive for broad market exposure  

**IMPACT**  
• Negative – Elevated macro risks, crude-driven inflation, and external vulnerabilities could pressure growth, currency, and equity markets

**URBAN COMPANY: AMBIT INITIATES SELL (12% DOWNSIDE)**

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• SELL rating with target price ₹97 (~12% downside)  
• Concerns over saturation in core India business  
• High cash burn in Instahelp segment  
• Current valuation factors in aggressive growth assumptions  

**BUSINESS STRUCTURE**  
• Core India (ex-Instahelp): ~75% of NTV and ~71% of revenue (primary profit driver)  
• Instahelp: Early-stage quick-service offering (~1% of revenue, high growth)  
• International & Native products: <25% contribution; treated as optionalities  

**BUSINESS DRIVERS**  
• Large addressable market (~₹5 lakh Cr; expected ~10–11% CAGR)  
• Highly unorganized sector (>90%) with low online penetration (~0.8%)  
• Growth driven by urbanization, nuclear families & higher workforce participation  

**CORE INDIA BUSINESS**  
• Strong historical growth (~20% CAGR in NTV FY23–26E)  
• High penetration (~51%) in top-8 cities → nearing saturation  
• Future growth dependent on smaller cities and higher usage frequency  
• Margin improvement driven by operating leverage, but limited further upside  

**INSTAHELP (KEY RISK AREA)**  
• Positioned as “quick commerce for services” (cleaning, house-help)  
• Rapid scale-up but significant losses (~₹115 Cr in last 3 quarters)  
• EBITDA margins deeply negative, though improving  
• Break-even expected only by FY31 due to competition and discounting  

**INTERNATIONAL & OTHER SEGMENTS**  
• UAE & Singapore showing strong growth and nearing breakeven  
• KSA JV still loss-making  
• Native products (RO, smart locks) scaling but not yet profitable  

**FINANCIAL OUTLOOK**  
• Revenue: ₹1,144 Cr → ₹2,380 Cr (FY25–28E)  
• Margins improving but overall profitability volatile  
• Strong cash position (~₹2,100 Cr) with ongoing cash burn  

**VALUATION**  
• CMP: ₹110 | Target: ₹97  
• Core business valued at ~35x FY28E EV/EBITDA  
• Implied expectations higher than realistic growth assumptions  

**RISKS**  
• Core market saturation limiting growth  
• High and prolonged cash burn in Instahelp  
• Competitive intensity in new segments  
• Overly optimistic market expectations  

**IMPACT**  
• Negative – Growth sustainability concerns, high cash burn, and rich valuation limit upside potential

**TRENT: MOTILAL OSWAL MAINTAINS BUY (29% UPSIDE, BUT NORMALIZATION PHASE)**

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• BUY rating with target price ₹4,350 (~29% upside)  
• Strong structural retail story with Zudio & Westside leadership  
• Entering earnings downgrade and productivity normalization phase  
• Future upside dependent on SSSG recovery rather than margin expansion  

**BUSINESS STRUCTURE**  
• Core formats: Zudio (value fashion) & Westside (mid-premium)  
• Optionality from Star (grocery JV) and Zara JV  
• Zudio scaling rapidly; Westside focusing on larger premium stores  

**GROWTH & STORE EXPANSION**  
• Revenue expected: ₹17,100 Cr → ₹28,400 Cr (FY25–28, ~18% CAGR)  
• Zudio stores: 80 (FY20) → ~888 (9MFY26)  
• Expansion shifting towards tier-2/3 markets  
• Store additions moderating as company enters consolidation phase  

**PRODUCTIVITY TRENDS**  
• Sales per sq ft declining (₹1,247 → ₹1,125/month)  
• Expected further 1–2% annual decline due to mix & competition  
• Cannibalization stabilizing but cluster-level growth remains moderate  
• Productivity below earlier “super-normal” levels  

**MARGINS & RETURNS**  
• EBITDA margin improved structurally (~9.3% → ~13.7%)  
• Margins expected to stabilize (~13.2%) going forward  
• PAT growth slower than EBITDA due to normalization and higher tax  
• Strong balance sheet moving to net cash by FY28  
• ROE moderating (~31–32% → ~23–24%)  

**BUSINESS TRENDS**  
• Strong demand for value fashion but rising competition  
• Expansion into smaller cities with lower productivity  
• Increasing competition from value fashion brands (Yousta, EasyBuy, etc.)  
• Growth increasingly dependent on same-store sales  

**VALUATION**  
• CMP: ₹3,366 | Target: ₹4,350  
• Trading at ~32x FY28E EBITDA and ~51x FY28E P/E  
• Premium valuation vs peers despite growth moderation  
• SOTP based valuation across Zudio, Westside, Star & Zara  

**RISKS**  
• Continued decline in store productivity  
• Slower store additions or weaker ramp-up in new markets  
• Intensifying competition in value fashion  
• Further earnings downgrades possible  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive (Long Term) – Strong brand and store economics, but near-term growth moderation and high valuation limit upside until productivity recovers

**MCX: HDFC SECURITIES MAINTAINS BUY (21% UPSIDE)**

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• BUY rating with revised target price ₹2,950 (~21% upside)  
• Strong growth driven by options expansion, product launches & regulatory tailwinds  
• Trading near peer valuations despite historical premium  
• Benefiting from rising commodity market participation  

**BUSINESS DRIVERS**

• Gold & silver contribute ~30% of options ADTV; bullion share ~57%

**VOLUME & REVENUE TRENDS**  
• Q4FY26 options notional: ~₹6.2 lakh Cr  
• Premium surged ~50% QoQ to ~₹11,200 Cr driven by higher P/N  
• Driven by volatility spike in crude & bullion (Middle East tensions)

**REGULATORY TAILWINDS**  
• Higher STT on equities vs stable CTT for commodities improves cost advantage

• Regulatory support to deepen commodity markets  

**STRUCTURAL OPPORTUNITIES**  
• Commodity indices (Metldex, Bulldex) to drive institutional participation  
• Potential entry of FPIs and institutional investors (currently underpenetrated)

• Long runway for growth as commodity derivatives market evolves  

**FINANCIAL OUTLOOK**

• Trading at ~40x/34x FY27E/FY28E EPS  
• Valuation pegged at ~43x FY28E EPS  

**RISKS**  
• Volume normalization as volatility reduces  
• Dependence on commodity price cycles  
• High sensitivity to trading activity levels  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Strong structural growth drivers, regulatory tailwinds, and expanding participation support long-term growth despite near-term normalization risk

reports.text:
**BAJAJ FINSERV: MOTILAL OSWAL INITIATES COVERAGE (NEUTRAL, 11% UPSIDE)**

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Initiated with Neutral rating; target price ₹1,900 (~11% upside)  
• Positioned as a tech-led financial services ecosystem (lending, insurance, investments, healthcare)  
• Consolidated FY26–28 CAGR: Revenue ~15%, PAT ~17%  
• RoE expected to sustain at ~13–14%  

**BUSINESS STRUCTURE**  
• Core subsidiaries: Bajaj Finance (BAF), Bajaj General Insurance (BGen), Bajaj Life Insurance (BLife)  
• Emerging platforms: BFH (health), BFD (marketplace), BFAMC (AMC)  
• Core value driven by BAF; insurance adds margin stability; digital platforms provide optionality  

**SUBSIDIARY DRIVERS**  
• **BAF (Lending)**:  
  – AUM ~₹4.8–4.9 lakh Cr; strong historical growth (PAT CAGR ~34%)  
  – 110M+ customers with significant cross-sell opportunity  
  – AI-led underwriting to reduce credit cost and improve productivity  
  – Asset quality stable (GNPA <1.4%, NNPA <0.5% expected)  

• **BGen (General Insurance)**:  
  – ~7.1% market share; strong combined ratio  
  – Health segment now ~38% of GWP  
  – FY26–28E: GWP CAGR ~12%, PAT CAGR ~16%  

• **BLife (Life Insurance)**:  
  – Strong APE/VNB growth; improving VNB margins (~16.4%)  
  – Shift towards protection and traditional products  
  – FY26–28E: APE CAGR ~15%, VNB CAGR ~19%  

**EMERGING BUSINESSES**  
• BFH: Health ecosystem with large provider network; breakeven over medium term  
• BFD: Digital marketplace + BFSI tech services; nearing PAT breakeven  
• BFAMC: Growing AMC platform with ~₹30,000 Cr AUM  
• Currently ~1% of revenue but offers long-term optionality  

**FINANCIAL OUTLOOK**  
• FY26–28E Revenue: ₹1.56L Cr → ₹2.05L Cr  
• PAT: ₹10,000 Cr → ₹13,700 Cr  
• EPS: ₹63 → ₹86  
• Stable RoE profile ~13–14%  

**VALUATION (SOTP BASIS)**  
• Total SOTP value: ~₹3.8 lakh Cr  
• After 20% holdco discount: ~₹3.0 lakh Cr equity value  
• Implied valuation at ~22x FY28E P/E  

**RISKS**  
• Credit stress in unsecured/MSME lending (BAF)  
• Regulatory risks for NBFCs and transition dynamics  
• Insurance cycle risks (pricing, claims, health inflation)  
• Execution risk in scaling digital/emerging platforms  

**IMPACT**  
• Neutral – Strong diversified franchise with steady growth, but limited upside due to current valuation and holdco discount

reports.text:
**STERLITE TECHNOLOGIES: SUNIDHI INITIATES COVERAGE (AI FIBRE SUPER-CYCLE PLAY)**

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Initiated with BUY rating; target price ₹240 (~39% upside)  
• Pivoting from traditional fibre player to AI-ready digital infra provider  
• Positioned to benefit from global fibre “supercycle” driven by AI data centres  
• Targeting hyperscalers like AWS, Google, Meta & Microsoft  

**BUSINESS DRIVERS**  
• AI data centres require ~36x higher fibre vs traditional systems  
• Strong tailwinds from FTTx, Data Centres & 5G growth  
• Global DC capex expected ~$570 bn by 2026; IT capex ~$2.8 tn by 2029  
• Increasing demand for high-density fibre and next-gen connectivity  

**COMPETITIVE POSITIONING**  
• India’s leading integrated optical fibre & cable manufacturer  
• ~50 mfkm fibre capacity, ~40 mfkm OFC capacity  
• ~8% global capacity share (ex-China)  
• Strong innovation pipeline with 780+ patents  

**BUSINESS MIX & GROWTH**  
• Enterprise & Data Centre segment ~20% of revenue (target ~30%)  
• Attach rate improved from ~0% (FY20) to ~22% (FY25)  
• North America share increased to ~36% (key growth driver)  
• STL Digital adds software + AI + cloud capabilities  

**FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE**  
• Q3 FY26 Revenue: ₹1,257 Cr (↑ 26% YoY | ↑ 21.6% QoQ)  
• EBITDA: ₹120 Cr (Margin: 9.5%)  
• 9M EBITDA Margin: 11.5% (vs 9.7% YoY)  
• Order Book: ₹5,330 Cr; 9M order inflow ₹4,260 Cr (↑ 40% YoY)  
• Net Debt: ~₹1,330 Cr; Net Debt/EBITDA: 2.58x  

**INDUSTRY TRENDS**  
• Fibre prices rising globally due to preform supply constraints  
• Strong demand from hyperscalers and AI infra buildout  
• Shift towards multi-year capacity contracts by global players  
• Emerging technologies: hollow-core fibre, multi-core fibre  

**FINANCIAL OUTLOOK**  
• Revenue CAGR: ~24% (FY26–FY28E)  
• EBITDA CAGR: ~55% with margin expansion to ~18.5%  
• PAT CAGR: ~304%; FY28E PAT ₹642 Cr  
• EPS: ₹6 (FY27E) → ₹12 (FY28E)  

**RISKS**  
• Ongoing US litigation (~$96.5 mn exposure)  
• Dependence on China for key raw materials (Germanium)  
• Potential slowdown in AI capex cycle  
• Competitive pressure from global and Chinese players  

**VALUATION**  
• CMP: ₹173 | Target: ₹240  
• FY27E P/E: ~28.6x | FY28E P/E: ~14.4x  
• Valuation based on 20x FY28E EPS  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Strong positioning in AI-driven fibre demand cycle with improving margins and growth visibility, though execution and macro risks remain

**DEFENCE PRODUCTION**

KESAR PETROPRODUCTS LTD

Bharat Connect Conference Notes (Rising Stars 2026)

Outlook:
Stable demand from packaging and plastic industry. Utilization ~65–70% gives growth without major capex. Focus on high-margin color & additives may improve margins (~8–12%). Risk: crude-linked raw material volatility.

Business:
Manufactures polymer additives & masterbatches for packaging, pipes, films, molding. B2B model.

Capacity:

Raw Materials:
PP, PE, CaCO₃, TiO₂ – linked to crude

Demand:
Packaging, FMCG, agri films, pipes

Customers:

Triggers:

• Domestic expansion
• Export opportunity
• Operating leverage

Key Takeaway:
Steady growth story with margin upside, dependent on product mix and raw material trend.

**Nomura – Sagility (Buy, Target 55)**  

- Buy with price target 55  
- Dual theme: value plus growth  
- Outsourcing demand and AI efficiency driving momentum  
- Vertically integrated healthcare‑focused tech player  
- Payer margin pressure boosts outsourcing, aiding growth  
- Well‑positioned to benefit from AI adoption  
- 20% EPS CAGR expected for FY26–FY28

**BofA – IT (Neutral, Selective)**  

- Growth trend expected to hold in Q4  
- Q4 exit growth rates likely better than last year  
- Revenue guidance seen with cushion for Middle East uncertainty  
- Fx provides ample cushion for profitability and EPS in FY27  
- Infosys expected to outperform Wipro in operating performance

**Jefferies – Real Estate (Launch‑Driven Quarter)**  

- Launch‑driven quarter with strong sales momentum  
- Jan–Feb sales velocity improved in Top‑7 city residential markets  
- February likely highest ever by volumes  
- Expect listed developers to report 10–15% Q4 sales growth  
- FY26E sales growth guided at 20%+  
- Iran‑conflict sentiment may soften FY27 guidance tone  
- Valuations near long‑term lows on PNAV and sub‑20x reported PE

**Jefferies – Housing Finance NBFC (AHFCs)**  

- Housing Finance valuations have reset lower  
- Risk/reward looks favourable for affordable HFCs  
- Disbursements soft at lower end; Q4–YTD momentum improved  
- Lower rates should support demand stability

- AHFC growth moderated, valuation premia normalised

**Jefferies – Bharat Forge (Buy, Target 2150)**  

- Buy call with price target of 2150

**CLSA – Mphasis (Hold, Target 2219)**  

- Lacking consistency in growth and performance

- Recent deal wins offset by leakage in existing business  
- Stable mid‑teen EBIT margin, but limited operating leverage  
- Fair‑value based, hence Hold with 2219 target

**MOSL View – Bajaj Finserv (Neutral, Target 1900)**  

- One‑stop financial‑services ecosystem play  
- Bajaj Finance: India’s leading non‑bank lender  
- Bajaj General Insurance: Scaling with disciplined underwriting  
- Bajaj Life: Turnaround story in life insurance  
- Emerging subsidiaries cover wider financial needs  
- Ecosystem supports cross‑selling and wallet share  
- Valuation already reflects strong execution  
- Target 1900 priced for steady credit, insurance margin, and capital‑allocation delivery

**AEQUS: AEROSPACE CORE + ELECTRONICS OPTIONALITY, BUT CAPEX-HEAVY STORY**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• BUY; TP ₹145 (~15% upside)  
• Strong aerospace engine + high-growth electronics optionality  
• Near-term earnings weak; valuation driven by future scale  

**BUSINESS OVERVIEW**  
• Precision engineering player with 2 segments:  
  • Aerospace (core, high-margin)  
  • Consumer (toys + electronics, high-growth optionality)  
• Vertically integrated aerospace ecosystem (Belagavi SEZ)  
• Global clients: Airbus, Boeing, Safran, Collins  

**CORE THESIS**  
• Aerospace = stable, high-margin growth engine  
• Electronics = high-beta re-rating lever (China+1)  
• Strong export-led model (~97% USD-linked revenue)  
• However, heavy capex + weak PAT near-term  

**AEROSPACE BUSINESS (CORE ENGINE)**  
• 5,200+ SKUs across structures, engines, landing gear  
• Tier-1 supplier to Airbus & Boeing  
• Long relationships (8–15 years)  
• Order book: ~$814 mn (~3–4 years visibility)  
• EBITDA margin: ~26–27% (steady)  
• Strong India sourcing tailwind from OEMs  

**CONSUMER / ELECTRONICS (OPTIONALITY)**  
• Legacy: toys (Hasbro, Mattel), cookware  
• New growth: smart devices & laptop components  
• Large global electronics client (Apple ecosystem proxy)  
• Capacity: 218 CNC machines; 0.3 mn sqft facility  
• Utilisation: ~31% → potential 70–80% by FY29–30  
• Margin: negative → ~10% by FY30  

**CHINA+1 TAILWIND**  
• Global brands shifting sourcing to India/Vietnam  
• Apple ecosystem expansion driving precision demand  
• Potential trajectory similar to EMS scaling models  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue CAGR: >30% (FY26–30E)  
• EBITDA CAGR: ~46%  
• Aerospace stable; consumer turnaround drives growth  
• PAT: negative till FY28 (due to high depreciation + interest)  

**MARGINS & STRUCTURE**  
• Aerospace: high margin, stable  
• Consumer: currently loss-making → improving with scale  
• Consolidated EBITDA expansion as utilisation improves  

**CAPEX & BALANCE SHEET**  
• Sustained capex: ₹4.5–5.5 bn annually  
• High CNC capacity expansion  
• Rising debt + lease liabilities  
• FCF: weak/negative in near term  

**WORKING CAPITAL**  
• High due to aerospace inventory cycles  
• Additional WC needed for consumer scale-up  
• Cash flow pressure during growth phase  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**  
• Valued on EV/EBITDA (no P/E due to losses)  
• Implied ~40x FY28E EV/EBITDA  
• Premium reflects future growth expectations  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Electronics segment scaling (utilisation ramp-up)  
• Aerospace order conversion & sourcing shift to India  
• Margin improvement in consumer business  
• Successful execution of China+1 opportunity  

**RISKS**  
• Execution risk in electronics ramp-up  
• High capex → balance sheet stress  
• Competitive pressure (China/Vietnam players)  
• Aerospace pricing pressure from OEMs  
• Continued PAT losses delaying re-rating  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Dual-engine story: stable aerospace + optionality-driven electronics  
• Current valuation already factors in future success  
• Near-term = investment phase, long-term = scale payoff  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Positive (High Risk–High Reward)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Aequs is a long-term precision manufacturing play with strong aerospace fundamentals and significant electronics optionality, but heavy capex, weak near-term earnings, and elevated valuations make execution the key determinant of returns

**DEE DEVELOPMENT ENGINEERS: INTEGRATION-LED HIGH GROWTH ENGINEERING PLAY**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Positive; TP ₹340 (~36% upside)  
• Strong earnings CAGR (Revenue ~30%, PAT ~61%)  
• Backward integration + capacity expansion driving margins  

**BUSINESS OVERVIEW**  
• Specialist in process & critical piping solutions  
• Key sectors: Power, Oil & Gas, Industrial infra  
• Moving into high-spec alloy seamless pipes (value chain expansion)  

**CORE THESIS**  
• Strong energy capex cycle (thermal + oil & gas) tailwind  
• Backward integration reduces dependency & boosts margins  
• Order book provides multi-year visibility  
• Operating leverage + automation driving profitability  

**ORDER BOOK & VISIBILITY**  
• Order book: ~₹1,900 Cr (~2 years visibility)  
• Power segment: ~₹983 Cr  
• Oil & Gas: ~₹636 Cr  
• Landmark order: ₹364 Cr HRSG piping (US OEM)  
• Thermal pipeline: 38 projects bid; ₹4,000–5,000 Cr opportunity  

**CAPACITY EXPANSION (ANJAR PLANT)**  
• Fabrication capacity: +30,000 MTPA (operational)  
• Seamless pipes: 7,000 TPA (new facility)  
• Location advantage: proximity to Kandla port → lower logistics cost  
• Focus: export-oriented oil & gas orders  

**BACKWARD INTEGRATION (KEY DRIVER)**  
• Capex: ₹90 Cr (seamless pipe facility)  
• Revenue potential: ~₹450 Cr at peak utilisation  
• Benefits:  
  • Reduced supplier dependency  
  • Better quality control  
  • Margin expansion via in-house sourcing  

**MARGIN EXPANSION LEVERS**  
• Logistics savings (port proximity)  
• Seamless pipe integration  
• Automation (3x throughput potential)  
• Operating leverage (fixed cost absorption)  
• OPM: ~12.9% → ~18–20% (medium-term)  

**GROWTH GUIDANCE**  
• FY27 targets:  
  • Revenue: ₹1,500 Cr  
  • Order inflow: ₹2,000 Cr  
  • EBITDA margin: ~17–18%  
• FY30 potential:  
  • Revenue: ~₹2,500 Cr  
  • Margin: ~18–20%  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue: ₹827 Cr → ₹1,800 Cr (FY25–28E)  
• OPM: ~15% → ~19.5%  
• PAT: ₹43.6 Cr → ₹181.5 Cr  
• EPS: ₹6.7 → ₹27.7  
• RoNW: ~9% → ~24%  
• RoCE: ~7% → ~13.8%  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**  
• P/E: ~16x FY27E → ~9x FY28E  
• Attractive vs growth + margin profile  
• Re-rating driven by integration + scale  

**BALANCE SHEET & CAPEX**  
• Major capex cycle largely completed  
• Existing capacity supports ₹2,500 Cr revenue  
• Limited incremental capex till FY30  

**WORKING CAPITAL DYNAMICS**  
• Structurally high WC (inventory-heavy model)  
• ~6 months inventory required  
• Growth → higher WC requirement  
• FCF conversion constrained in high growth phase  

**OTHER BUSINESS (BIOMASS DRAG)**  
• ₹30–36 Cr loss due to tariff cut (₹8 → ₹3/unit)  
• Mitigation: pellet integration  
• Expected breakeven FY26, profitability FY27  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Seamless pipe ramp-up  
• Thermal & oil & gas order inflows  
• Margin expansion via automation  
• Execution of large HRSG contracts  

**RISKS**  
• Cyclicality in power & oil & gas capex  
• Raw material (steel/alloy) volatility  
• Working capital pressure  
• Execution risk in new facilities  
• Geopolitical disruptions (export delays)  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Transition from EPC supplier → integrated piping + pipe manufacturer  
• Backward integration + automation = structural margin upgrade  
• Strong positioning in high-entry barrier segments  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Strong Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Dee Development Engineers is entering a high-growth, margin-expansion phase driven by capacity expansion, seamless pipe integration, and strong sector tailwinds, supporting a meaningful re-rating opportunity

**POWER GRID CORPORATION: STRONG PIPELINE, BUT RETURN PROFILE CAPPED**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Neutral; TP ₹302 (~0% upside)  
• Massive multi-trillion transmission opportunity  
• High capex cycle caps RoE expansion & dividend upside  

**BUSINESS OVERVIEW**  
• India’s largest transmission utility with regulated return model  
• Core business: inter-state transmission, HVDC systems, grid infra  
• Asset-heavy model with stable, predictable cash flows  

**CORE THESIS**  
• Structural beneficiary of India’s energy transition (RE integration)  
• Strong capex visibility for next decade  
• Execution risks easing (RoW, manpower, supply chain)  
• However, regulated returns limit upside despite growth  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue: ₹492bn → ₹563bn  
• EBITDA: ₹418bn → ₹467bn  
• EBITDA margin: ~83–85% (very high, stable)  
• PAT: ₹168bn → ₹189bn  
• EPS: ₹18.0 → ₹20.3 (~6–8% CAGR)  

**RETURN RATIOS**  
• RoE: ~17.4% → ~16.9% (gradual decline)  
• RoCE: ~9.5–9.6% (stable)  
• RoIC: ~11–11.5%  
• Growth driven by asset addition, not margin expansion  

**CAPEX & CAPITALIZATION (KEY DRIVER)**  
• FY26 capex upgraded: ₹320bn → ₹350bn  
• Capitalization: ₹220bn → ₹250bn  
• FY27/FY28 capex: ₹350bn / ₹450bn  
• Continuous upward revisions → strong execution visibility  

**LONG-TERM PIPELINE (MEGA OPPORTUNITY)**  
• Total transmission opportunity: ~₹15 trillion  
  • RE integration: ~₹7.9T  
  • Brahmaputra basin: ~₹4T  
  • International HVDC: ~₹3–4T  
• PWGR potential share: ~₹6–7.5T over 10–12 years  

**HVDC – KEY GROWTH ENGINE**  
• Large upcoming projects:  
  • Bikaner–Begunia HVDC  
  • Barmer–South Kalamb HVDC  
• Mega themes:  
  • One Sun One World One Grid (OSOWOG)  
  • Cross-border interconnections (Middle East, SE Asia)  
• High-value, long-duration assets aligned with PWGR expertise  

**EXECUTION IMPROVEMENTS**  
• RoW issues easing via policy reforms  
• Skilled manpower pipeline (1,400+ technicians/year)  
• Transformer/reactor supply constraints easing  
• Faster execution → better capitalization visibility  

**BALANCE SHEET & CASH FLOW**  
• Net D/E: ~1.4x → ~1.2x  
• Net debt/EBITDA: ~3.0x → ~2.3x  
• Strong operating cash flow: ₹400bn+ → ₹570bn  
• FCF positive but moderated due to heavy capex  

**DIVIDEND PROFILE**  
• DPS: ₹9.6 → ₹11 (FY26–28E)  
• Yield: ~3.2% → ~3.7%  
• Payout: ~52–54%  
• Risk: dividend growth capped due to capex needs  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**

• Profitability still evolving → key monitorable  
• Growth vs valuation mismatch currently  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Neutral  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Urban Company offers a strong long-term structural story in a large, underpenetrated market, but current valuations already price in much of the growth, limiting near-term upside

reports.text:
**CHEMICAL SECTOR: COST SHOCK & MARGIN COMPRESSION PHASE**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Brent >$100 + LNG/LPG disruption → sharp cost inflation  
• Freight disruption (Cape rerouting) → higher logistics cost  
• Sector entering margin compression cycle  

**COST PRESSURE DRIVERS**  
• Feedstock inflation (benzene, toluene, methanol, naphtha)  
• Higher fuel, freight & insurance costs  
• Export viability at risk for low-margin products  

**SECTOR DYNAMICS**  
• Commodity chemicals: most impacted (margin squeeze)  
• Specialty chemicals: relatively resilient (pricing power, contracts)  
• Export-oriented low-value products under pressure  

**PREFERRED STRATEGY**  
• Defensive stance  
• Focus on:  
  • High-value product mix  
  • Strong balance sheet  
  • Domestic-focused players  
  • Lower Middle East feedstock dependency  

**TOP PICKS (AXIS VIEW)**  
• Navin Fluorine – BUY | TP ₹7,400  
• Dhanuka Agritech – BUY | TP ₹1,600  

**OTHER COVERAGE**  
• Aarti Industries – BUY | TP ₹530  
• Archean Chemical – BUY | TP ₹515  
• Apcotex – HOLD | TP ₹400  
• Camlin Fine Science – HOLD | TP ₹170  
• PI Industries – HOLD | TP ₹3,230  
• NOCIL – HOLD | TP ₹150  

**IMPACT CLASSIFICATION**  
• High Impact:  
  • Aarti Industries, Apcotex, NOCIL, Camlin Fine  
• Lower Impact / Resilient:  
  • Navin Fluorine, PI Industries, Dhanuka Agritech  

**FINANCIAL OUTLOOK (SELECT)**  
• Navin Fluorine: PAT ₹685 → ₹1,221 Cr; ROE ~17–20%  
• PI Industries: PAT ₹1,266 → ₹1,909 Cr; stable returns  
• Dhanuka: PAT ₹269 → ₹428 Cr; strong ROCE ~22–27%  
• Archean: PAT ₹175 → ₹601 Cr; margin expansion potential  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Easing of Strait of Hormuz disruption  
• Correction in crude & freight costs  
• Successful cost pass-through  
• Margin recovery visibility  

**RISKS**  
• Sustained crude >$100  
• Prolonged LNG/LPG shortage  
• Elevated freight/insurance costs  
• Weak export demand & agri stress  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Sector split widening: specialty vs commodity divergence  
• Margin pressure near-term, structural story intact  
• Stock selection critical in current environment  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Negative (near-term) | Selective Positive (specialty players)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• The chemical sector is entering a cost-driven margin compression phase, where only high-quality, specialty players with pricing power and strong balance sheets are likely to outperform

**NAZARA TECHNOLOGIES: ACQUISITION-LED EARNINGS RE-RATING**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Upgraded to BUY; TP ₹336 (~42% upside)  
• Earnings sharply upgraded post BT Games + BP Systems acquisition  
• Transitioning into scaled global casual gaming platform  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue: ₹16.2bn → ₹40.5bn (FY25–28, ~27.7% CAGR)  
• EBITDA: ₹5.0bn → ₹6.4bn (FY27–28E)  
• EBITDA margin: ~9.4% → ~15.8–15.9%  
• PAT: sharp recovery to ₹2.75bn by FY28  

**EARNINGS RESET**  
• FY27/28 sales revised up by ~62% / ~85%  
• EBITDA up ~64% / ~74%  
• EPS up ~28% / ~57%  

**STRATEGIC ACQUISITION (BT + BP)**  
• Acquiring 50% stake (Spain-based gaming platform)  
• Portfolio: 17 games | 375mn downloads | 22mn MAUs  
• Strong ad-driven monetisation (~95% revenue)  

**BUSINESS MODEL ADVANTAGE**  
• BP Systems = proprietary user acquisition (UA) engine  
• 40–50% installs driven internally → lower CAC, higher LTV  
• AI-led game development (cycle reduced to ~2–3 months)  

**GROWTH VISIBILITY**  
• BT+BP revenue: ~₹12.1bn (FY27E) → ~₹18.6bn (FY28E)  
• EBITDA margin: ~18–20%  
• Casual gaming becomes core growth driver  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**  
• Trades ~31.6x FY28E EPS | ~12.9x EV/EBITDA  
• Re-rating driven by scale + improved margins  
• BT+BP valued at ~1.5x FY28E sales  

**BALANCE SHEET & RETURNS**  
• Funded via internal cash + earn-out structure  
• RoE/RoCE improving to ~7.5% / ~9% by FY28  
• Near-term FCF pressure due to investment phase  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Closure of BT/BP deal (2–3 months)  
• Scaling of casual gaming portfolio  
• UA engine integration across Nazara ecosystem  
• Opening BP platform to 3rd-party developers  

**RISKS**  
• Integration risk (large overseas acquisition)  
• Dependence on ad monetisation (Meta/Google policies)  
• Short-term FCF pressure  
• Execution risk in scaling new vertical  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Shift from fragmented gaming portfolio → platform-led model  
• UA + content + distribution integration = strong moat  
• Earnings visibility significantly improved  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Strong Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Nazara is entering a new growth phase driven by a transformative acquisition, with sharp earnings upgrades, improving margins, and platform-level scalability supporting a potential re-rating cycle

**KIRLOSKAR PNEUMATIC: PRODUCT-LED RE-RATING STORY**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Strong guidance: ₹18bn (FY26) → ₹21bn (FY27) revenue  
• EBIT margin ~20%; long-term ~19% sustainable  
• TP ₹1,556 (~48% upside); re-rating driven by mix shift  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue: ₹16.4bn → ₹24.5bn (FY25–28, ~14% CAGR)  
• EBITDA: ₹2.9bn → ₹4.7bn (~21% CAGR)  
• PAT: ₹2.15bn → ₹3.47bn (~17.5% CAGR)  
• PAT margin: ~12.9% → ~14.0%  

**BUSINESS MIX TRANSFORMATION**  
• Compressors to contribute ~80–82% (core business)  
• Precision Engineering: ~18–20% (~₹3.5–4bn)  
• Shift toward shorter-cycle, product-led revenues  

**CORE GROWTH DRIVER (AIR COMPRESSORS)**  
• 15–20% growth guided for FY27  
• Tezcatlipoca platform scaling across capacity bands  
• Export push + dry screw replacement opportunity  

**SEGMENT TRENDS**  
• Refrigeration: Near-term slowdown (~7–8% growth)  
• Gas Compression: Structural decline (CNG slowdown)  
• Services/O&M: Cushioning revenues (annuity stream)  

**OPTIONALITY LEVER (ZEPHYROS)**  
• Large opportunity: ₹50bn within ₹250bn AC market  
• Ammonia-based tech → higher efficiency, ESG compliant  
• Pilot phase now; meaningful scale-up post FY28  

**ORDER BOOK & DEMAND**  
• Weak large project pipeline (O&G, petrochem)  
• Less lumpiness → smoother execution  
• Near-term volatility in order inflows possible  

**BALANCE SHEET**  
• Near-zero net debt  
• Strong FCF: ₹1.37bn → ₹2.84bn (FY25–28)  
• ROE/ROCE improving: ~21–26% range  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**  
• Trades ~24x/20x FY27/28E EPS  
• Target multiple: 32x → premium justified by mix shift  
• Re-rating driven by product + margin expansion  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Tezcatlipoca scale-up & export traction  
• Precision Engineering execution & margins  
• Zephyros commercialization success  
• Pickup in compressor order inflows  

**RISKS**  
• Weak capex cycle (O&G, petrochem, CNG)  
• Delay in Zephyros ramp-up  
• Lumpiness in Precision Engineering orders  
• Margin slippage vs ~20% guidance  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Transition from project-led → product + services model  
• Higher margin, lower cyclicality business mix  
• Optionality (Zephyros) adds long-term upside  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Strong Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Kirloskar Pneumatic is evolving into a structurally stronger, product-driven engineering play with improving margins, strong cash flows, and meaningful optionality—supporting a potential re-rating cycle

**INDIA MACRO: OIL SHOCK RESET (EMKAY VIEW)**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Brent assumption raised to ~$80/bbl (FY27E); spike to ~$125 in near term  
• GDP cut to 6.6% (–40 bps); CPI raised to 4.3% (+30 bps)  
• Clear shift: inflation ↑, growth ↓ (stagflation-lite risk)  

**EXTERNAL BALANCE IMPACT**  
• CAD/GDP: 1.3% → 1.7% (base case)  
• Stress case ($100 oil): CAD >2.4%, BoP deficit >$85bn  
• Extreme case ($130 oil): CAD ~3.7%, BoP deficit ~$145bn  

**ENERGY SHOCK TRANSMISSION**  
• ~90% oil import dependence  
• Strait of Hormuz critical:  
  • ~45–46% crude imports  
  • ~55% LNG imports  
• Gas curtailment already visible (~31% volumes cut)  

**OMC & FISCAL DYNAMICS**  
• OMC under-recoveries >₹3 lakh Cr annually  
• Required fuel price hikes:  
  • Diesel: ~42–43%  
  • Petrol: ~18–19%  
• Full govt absorption → fiscal hit ~1–1.1% of GDP  

**POLICY RESPONSE (BASE CASE)**  
• Shared burden model (Govt + OMCs + consumers)  
• OMC hit: ~0.35% of GDP  
• CPI impact: ~30–35 bps  
• Fiscal hit: ~0.4–0.5% of GDP  

**RBI & MARKETS**  
• INR depreciation likely tolerated (~USD/INR → 96)  
• 10Y G-Sec yield ~6.95%  
• Focus on FX intervention vs rate hikes  

**MACRO LINKAGES**  
• GCC exposure:  
  • ~38–40% remittances  
  • ~13% exports  
  • ~5–9% FDI  
• Risk of spillover via remittances & trade  

**SECTORAL IMPLICATIONS**  
• Negative:  
  • OMCs (margin uncertainty)  
  • Energy-intensive industries  
  • Fertilizers, consumption (cost pressure)  
• Positive:  
  • Upstream oil & gas  
  • Gas producers  
  • Exporters (INR depreciation benefit)  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Brent trajectory vs $80 base case  
• Strait of Hormuz reopening timeline  
• Fuel price hikes / subsidy decisions  
• RBI stance on INR & bond yields  

**RISKS**  
• Crude sustaining >$100/bbl  
• Deeper gas shortages → industrial slowdown  
• Fiscal slippage crowding out capex  
• Weakening remittances/export flows from GCC  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• India highly sensitive to oil shocks due to import dependence  
• Policy balancing act between inflation, growth & fiscal stability  
• Market direction hinges on oil normalization timeline  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Negative for macro stability | Mixed for equities  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• The oil shock creates a complex macro trade-off—while growth slows and external balances weaken, policy calibration and sectoral divergence (losers vs beneficiaries) will define market outcomes in the near to medium term

**IRAN CONFLICT: ENERGY SHOCK VS GROWTH SHOCK (MACRO STRATEGY)**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Largest oil supply disruption via Strait of Hormuz  
• Markets pricing inflation shock, not full growth shock  
• Elevated energy prices → downside risk to global growth  

**MACRO IMPACT**  
• +10% oil → GDP ↓ ~0.1%+, CPI ↑ ~0.2pp  
• Europe & Asia ex-China most impacted  
• Growth slowdown + sticky inflation risk  

**ENERGY MARKET DYNAMICS**  
• Brent elevated; could normalize to ~$70 (4Q26) if disruption eases  
• LNG supply disruption delays global supply wave  
• Structural support to gas prices medium-term  

**POLICY RESPONSE**  
• Fed cuts delayed (Sep, Dec expected)  
• ECB/BoE likely “higher for longer”  
• Tools: SPR releases, sanctions easing, fiscal measures  

**MARKET POSITIONING**  
• Markets underpricing growth shock risk  
• If prolonged disruption:  
  • Equities & cyclicals vulnerable  
  • Bond yields may fall (growth scare)  
  • Safe havens (JPY) outperform  

**SECTORAL IMPLICATIONS**  
• Winners:  
  • Energy (oil & gas)  
  • Inflation hedges (commodities)  
  • Safe-haven assets  
• Losers:  
  • Cyclicals, global trade-sensitive sectors  
  • High energy-import economies  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Strait of Hormuz flow data & convoy success  
• Oil/LNG price trajectory  
• Central bank policy shifts  
• Conflict duration & escalation signals  

**RISKS**  
• Prolonged disruption → oil spike beyond historical highs  
• LNG supply delays → sustained high gas prices  
• Policy/political uncertainty (US, Middle East)  
• Underestimated global growth slowdown  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Current market narrative incomplete (inflation-only view)  
• Growth shock risk is the real tail risk  
• Energy markets driving global macro direction  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Negative for global growth | Positive for energy assets  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• The Iran conflict represents a high-impact macro event where markets are underpricing growth risks—if energy disruptions persist, the narrative could shift from inflation shock to a broader global slowdown with significant implications for equities and asset allocation

**INOX INDIA: LNG ECOSYSTEM – STRUCTURAL COMPOUNDER**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• BUY; TP ₹1,610 (~40% upside)  
• Valuation: ~40x P/E, ~30x EV/EBITDA (premium justified by growth)  
• Strong CAGR: Revenue/EBITDA/PAT ~18–19% (FY25–28E)  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• FY28E Revenue: ₹21.5 bn | EBITDA: ₹4.7 bn (~22% margin)  
• PAT margin: ~17.9%  
• RoE: ~24–30% | RoCE: ~26–37%  
• Net cash balance sheet (negative net debt)  

**LNG MEGA OPPORTUNITY**  
• LNG ~10% of India’s energy mix → long runway  
• LNG trucks: ~30% cheaper vs diesel + long range (1,000+ km)  
• Infrastructure gap:  
  • India: ~49 stations (25–27 active)  
  • China: ~5,000 stations  
➡️ Massive infra build-out opportunity  

**INOX POSITIONING**  
• LNG value-chain contributes ~26% of revenue  
• Products: tanks, fueling infra, mini-LNG terminals  
• Beneficiary of OEM tie-ups (Tata, Eicher) + govt push  

**DIVERSIFIED GROWTH DRIVERS**  
• Industrial gases (core demand)  
• Cryo-scientific applications  
• New themes: data centres, semiconductors, aerospace  

**BALANCE SHEET STRENGTH**  
• Net cash company (–28% to –48% net D/E)  
• Strong cash conversion (CFO/EBITDA 43–76%)  
• Low capex intensity → scalable growth  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**  
• Premium multiples vs peers  
• Supported by:  
  • High margins  
  • Strong return ratios  
  • Long runway visibility  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• LNG trucking adoption + station ramp-up  
• Order inflows in LNG + cryogenic segments  
• Sustained 18–20% revenue growth with ~22% margins  
• New vertical scale-up (data centres, semicon, aerospace)  

**RISKS**  
• LNG price volatility impacting adoption  
• Slow infra rollout (stations)  
• Project delays (cryo/scientific)  
• Global LNG supply disruptions  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Pure play on LNG infra + cryogenic ecosystem  
• Early-stage penetration → long compounding runway  
• Diversification reduces single-theme dependency  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Strong Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Inox India is a high-quality structural growth play on India’s LNG transition, combining strong financials, early-mover advantage, and multi-sector exposure—justifying premium valuations for long-term compounding

**PRAJ INDUSTRIES: RECOVERY + OPTIONALITY PLAY**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Accumulate; TP ₹340 (~9% upside)  
• Valuation: ~28x/21x FY27/28E EPS  
• FY27 seen as recovery year  

**EARNINGS OUTLOOK**  
• Revenue: ₹35.9bn (FY27E, +11.9%) → ₹41.1bn (FY28E, +14.4%)  
• EBITDA margin: 5.8% → 8.9% → 9.9%  
• PAT: ₹0.75bn → ₹2.05bn → ₹2.76bn  
• ROE: ~5.5% → ~14% → ~17%  

**CORE BUSINESS SHIFT**  
• 1G ethanol moving from greenfield EPC →  
  • Brownfield upgrades  
  • Multi-feedstock solutions  
  • Services & exports  
• Leads to more stable, recurring revenue profile  

**GENX FACILITY (KEY SWING FACTOR)**  
• FY27 revenue: ₹4–5bn (breakeven)  
• High fixed cost: ₹900–1,100mn  
• Export-led mix (~75%)  
• Customer base doubled (8 → 16)  

**NEW GROWTH DRIVERS**  
• CBG: ₹10bn pipeline; 18–20 plants executed  
• SAF: Large global opportunity (₹15–20bn/project)  
• Isobutanol: Alternative diesel blending solution  
• Services: High-margin annuity stream emerging  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue CAGR: ~8% (FY25–28E)  
• Margin expansion to ~10% by FY28  
• ROCE: ~5% → ~16%  
• Low debt (~₹1.95bn), improving FCF  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• GenX ramp-up and breakeven execution  
• Ethanol blending policy (25–30%)  
• SAF blending mandate (India + global)  
• CBG scale-up and pipeline integration  
• Services & export order growth  

**RISKS**  
• Weak near-term order inflows  
• Domestic liquidity constraints  
• CBG infra/feedstock bottlenecks  
• Policy delays (ethanol, SAF, CBG)  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Transition from cyclical EPC → diversified bio-energy platform  
• Optionality from SAF, CBG, services not fully monetised yet  
• Recovery story + long-term energy transition play  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Positive (medium-term recovery + optionality)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Praj Industries is at an inflection point—near-term recovery led by GenX and margin normalisation, with significant long-term upside from bio-energy platforms like SAF, CBG, and services

**POWER GRID: CAPEX SUPER-CYCLE WITH REGULATED STABILITY**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• BUY maintained; TP ₹348 (~15% upside)  
• Strong regulated model with ~86–87% EBITDA margins  
• ROE stable at ~16–17%  

**EARNINGS & RETURNS**  
• EPS: ₹18.1 (FY27E), ₹19.8 (FY28E)  
• PAT CAGR: ~7–8% (steady, not high growth)  
• Dividend yield: ~3.3–3.7%  

**CAPEX VISIBILITY**  
• FY26 capex: ₹350+ bn (already achieved)  
• FY27: ₹370 bn | FY28: ₹450+ bn  
• Potential upside from TBCB wins  
• ~₹650 bn capitalization expected over next 2 years  

**PIPELINE STRENGTH**  
• Industry opportunity: ~₹15 trillion (FY27–36)  
• Company pipeline: ~₹1,480 bn (~₹1,000 bn executable)  
• Massive scale-up: ₹80–90 bn → ₹350 bn capex in ~3 years  

**EXECUTION IMPROVEMENT**  
• ROW issues easing (policy + compensation clarity)  
• Timelines reset to 30–36 months → better predictability  
• Supply constraints easing (transformers, manpower)  

**BALANCE SHEET**  
• Debt/EBITDA: ~3.4–3.5x  
• D/E: ~1.4x → ~1.6x (capex-led rise)  
• Backed by regulated returns → manageable leverage  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**  
• Trades ~15x FY28E EPS | ~2.4x BV  
• Premium justified by visibility + stability  
• Not a high-beta play → core utility holding  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• TBCB project wins  
• Capitalization pace vs guidance  
• SPV consolidation progress  
• Regulatory/ROW developments  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Pure play on India’s power transmission capex cycle  
• Earnings stability + high visibility → defensive compounder  
• Dividend + growth combo suits long-term allocation  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Power Grid offers a rare mix of predictable earnings, strong capex-led growth visibility, and steady dividends—making it a core portfolio utility rather than a high-growth bet

**PARADEEP PHOSPHATES: INTEGRATION-LED MARGIN EXPANSION STORY**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Initiated with Accumulate; TP ₹120 (~13% upside)  
• Valuation at ~10x FY28E EPS  
• Dividend yield: ~0.5–0.7%  

**BUSINESS POSITIONING**  
• 2nd largest private phosphatic fertilizer player in India  
• Capacity: 3.7 MTPA (granulation) + 0.8 MTPA urea  
• Total capacity to reach ~5 MTPA by FY29E  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
• Backward integration:  
  • Sulfuric acid: 2.0 → 3.03 MTPA  
  • Phosphoric acid: 0.5 → 1.0 MTPA  
• Integration benefit: ~₹350 Cr by FY27E  
• MCFL merger expands southern footprint + cross-selling  

**PRODUCT MIX SHIFT**  
• DAP share declining (28% → 17% → further down)  
• NPK share rising to ~58% by FY28E  
• Focus on higher-margin complex fertilizers  

**VOLUME OUTLOOK**  
• Sales: 3.8 MMT (FY25) → 4.6 MMT (FY28E)  
• Phosphatic fertilizers: ~3.1 MMT  
• Trading volumes: ~0.69 MMT  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue/EBITDA/PAT CAGR: ~10% / 18% / 23%  
• EBITDA margin: ~11.3% by FY28E  
• PAT margin: ~5.5%  
• ROE: ~11% → ~14% | ROCE: ~15%  

**BALANCE SHEET**  
• Net debt/equity: ~0.6x (FY28E)  
• Improving capital efficiency  

**SECTOR TAILWIND**  
• Shift from DAP/urea → NPK (nutrient efficiency focus)  
• NPK demand growing strongly  

**RISKS**  
• Raw material & freight volatility (global conflicts)  
• Monsoon dependency  
• Subsidy delays / policy changes  
• Currency fluctuations  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Core story = integration + mix improvement  
• Margin expansion driven by self-sufficiency in key inputs  
• MCFL merger adds scale + geographic diversification  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Positive (gradual re-rating potential)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Paradeep Phosphates offers a steady growth story with improving margins driven by backward integration and product mix shift, though upside remains moderate and execution-dependent

**INTERARCH BUILDING SOLUTIONS: CAPACITY LED GROWTH STORY**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Leading turnkey PEB player with ~200,000 MTPA capacity  
• Strong presence across AP, TN & Uttarakhand  
• Expanding into heavy structures + new geographies  

**CAPACITY EXPANSION**  
• New PEB plant: 40,000 MTPA (Kheda, Gujarat)  
• Heavy structures unit: 25,000 MTPA (Andhra Pradesh)  
• Strengthens end-to-end execution capability  

**BUSINESS MODEL**  
• Converts HR/CR steel into I-sections, beams & components  
• Offers full turnkey PEB solutions (design → manufacturing → execution)  
• In-house accessories enhance integration and margins  

**SEGMENT DYNAMICS**  
• PEB: High volume, faster execution (40–50mm plates)  
• Heavy structures: Complex, high-load projects (~125mm plates)  
• Lower throughput but higher value-added applications  

**DEMAND DRIVERS**  
• Warehousing (e-commerce), FMCG, cement, industrial capex  
• New growth areas: data centres, semiconductors, multi-storey buildings  
• Strong order pipeline with large project wins  

**FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE**  
• Revenue CAGR ~32% (FY22–25)  
• EBITDA margin: ~4% → ~9%  
• PAT growth strong; ROE ~19–20%, ROCE ~19–26%  

**BALANCE SHEET**  
• Net debt negative (cash-rich)  
• Strong free cash flow generation  
• Healthy EBITDA to OCF conversion  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Transition from pure PEB → higher-value heavy structures  
• Capacity expansion aligned with structural capex cycle  
• Integrated model + repeat orders = strong execution moat  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Interarch is evolving into a higher-value steel construction player, combining strong demand tailwinds with capacity expansion and a robust balance sheet, positioning it well for the ongoing infrastructure and industrial capex cycle

**ADITYA VISION: DEMAND-LED GROWTH WITH STORE EXPANSION**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• ICICI Sec maintains BUY; TP ₹600 (~31% upside from CMP ₹458)  
• Strong demand in RACs, fans, coolers → high-teen Q4 growth expected  
• Revenue/PAT CAGR (FY25–28E): ~19% / ~23%  

**DEMAND & CATEGORY TRENDS**  
• RAC: Strong demand + 7–9% price hike from Apr’26  
• LDA (fridge, WM, TV): Healthy traction; mix improving  
• Induction: Volume spike (gas shortage) but low earnings impact  
• Mobiles/Laptops: Weak due to chip shortage; price hikes ~5–7%  

**STORE EXPANSION**  
• 5 stores added in Q4; total ~197  
• On track to cross 200 stores by FY26 end  
• Expansion key driver for growth visibility  

**MARGIN & PROFITABILITY**  
• EBITDA margin stable at ~8.9%  
• Pre-stocked RAC inventory → near-term margin support  
• ROE improving: ~19.7% → ~21.3% (FY25–28E)  
• ROCE improving: ~13.4% → ~16%  

**BALANCE SHEET**  
• Net debt/EBITDA to decline: ~1.5x → ~0.6x  
• Strong asset turns (7.5x → 9.1x)  
• Improving free cash flow trajectory  

**RISKS**  
• Delay in store rollout  
• Input cost inflation / freight costs  
• Weak consumer demand or competition  
• Supply constraints (RAC, gas-related disruptions)  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Growth driven by store expansion + summer demand cycle  
• Operating leverage + improving balance sheet supports valuation  
• Near-term margin tailwind from inventory positioning  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Aditya Vision combines strong demand tailwinds with aggressive store expansion and improving financial metrics, offering a favorable growth trajectory with manageable risks

• AI is shaping up as the next structural growth engine for IT services, with leading Indian IT companies well-positioned to capture this opportunity

reports.text:
India Has Just 25 Days of Oil Reserves. Here’s Why It Matters

Among major economies, India has one of the lowest strategic oil reserves at just 25 days.

Compare that with Japan at 254 days and the United States at 189 days. The gap is significant.

This highlights a key vulnerability. India depends heavily on crude oil imports, making it sensitive to global price shocks and supply disruptions.

India’s $5 Trillion Gold Story:

• 125% of GDP
• 65% of household wealth
• Cyclical returns, long drawdowns
• Major capital locked outside the system

**MUTUAL FUND SCREENING: RISK-ADJUSTED OUTPERFORMERS**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Focus on actively managed equity mutual funds  
• Emphasis on consistency + quality of returns (not just absolute returns)  
• Based on robust multi-factor evaluation framework  

**SCREENING CRITERIA**  
• Minimum 3-year performance track record  
• Sharpe Ratio > 1 (efficient risk-return trade-off)  
• Sortino Ratio > 1 (better downside risk management)  
• Positive Information Ratio (consistent benchmark outperformance)  
• Alpha > 1 (genuine excess return generation)  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Filters out funds with volatile or unsustainable performance  
• Identifies process-driven fund managers with disciplined strategy  
• Focus shifts from returns → risk-adjusted wealth creation  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Positive (for long-term portfolio construction)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• This framework highlights high-quality mutual funds that deliver consistent, risk-efficient returns—ideal for building long-term, stable wealth rather than chasing short-term performance

When a market grows 6x, and one platform captures nearly all of it, that is worth paying attention to.

**POWER GRID: MOSL VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Rating: Neutral  
- Target Price: ₹302  
- Capex and capitalization guidance revised upward for FY26  

**CAPEX & GUIDANCE UPDATE**  
- FY26 Capex: ₹350 bn (↑ from ₹320 bn; vs earlier ₹280 bn)  
- FY26 Capitalization: ₹250 bn (↑ from ₹220 bn; vs earlier ₹200 bn)  
- FY27/28 Guidance unchanged:  
  • FY27: ₹300 bn / ₹350 bn  
  • FY28: ₹370 bn / ₹450 bn  

**GROWTH OUTLOOK**  
- Capex based on current order visibility; further wins may drive upgrades  
- Strong multi-trillion transmission opportunity unfolding in India  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Improved execution (ROW resolution) supporting higher capitalization  
- Large pipeline ensures long-term growth visibility  
- Incremental upside dependent on new project awards  

**VALUATION VIEW**  
- Balanced risk-reward at current levels  
- Strong fundamentals but limited near-term rerating triggers  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Power Grid remains a **high-visibility utility growth story**, with capex momentum improving, but near-term upside depends on fresh order inflows and valuation comfort

**POWER GRID: HSBC VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Rating: Hold  
- Target Price: ₹290  
- Capex & capitalization guidance raised; scope for further upgrades  

**GROWTH OUTLOOK**  
- Large transmission TAM of ~₹15 tn over next decade  
- Opportunity likely back-ended (execution skewed to later years)  

**NEW OPPORTUNITIES**  
- Intra-state transmission (intra-STS) expansion  
- BESS (Battery Energy Storage Systems) emerging as new growth avenue  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Strong long-term visibility driven by energy transition  
- Additional optionality from storage and intra-state projects  
- However, near-term growth may be moderate due to back-ended pipeline  

**VALUATION VIEW**  
- Limited upside at current levels  
- Balanced risk-reward despite improving capex outlook  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Power Grid remains a **steady long-term infrastructure play**, with new opportunities emerging, but near-term upside constrained due to valuation and timing of growth execution

**POWER GRID: KOTAK INSTITUTIONAL EQUITIES VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Rating: Reduce  
- Target Price: ₹300  
- Valuations seen as stretched despite strong growth visibility  

**CAPEX & GUIDANCE UPDATE**  
- FY26 Capex: ₹350 bn (↑ from ₹320 bn)  
- FY26 Capitalization: ₹250 bn (↑ from ₹220 bn)  
- Improvement driven by resolution of ROW (Right of Way) issues  
- FY27–28 guidance maintained  

**LONG-TERM OPPORTUNITY**  
- CEA estimates ~₹7.9 tn transmission capex for non-fossil capacity till FY36  
- Additional opportunities:  
  • Undersea projects: ~₹3 tn  
  • Brahmaputra basin: ~₹4 tn  
- Targeting ₹400 bn capex / ₹350 bn capitalization annually (FY29–36)  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Strong, visible long-term growth runway  
- Beneficiary of renewable energy transition and grid expansion  
- Execution improving with ROW bottlenecks easing  

**VALUATION CONCERN**  
- Trading at ~16x P/E → considered full valuation  
- Limited upside despite strong fundamentals  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral to Negative  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Power Grid offers **high visibility, stable growth**, but elevated valuations cap near-term upside, leading to a cautious stance despite strong structural tailwinds

**COAL INDIA: NUVAMA VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Rating: Reduce  
- Target Price: ₹384  
- Growth narrative seen weakening amid supply-demand mismatch  

**DEMAND & PRICING OUTLOOK**  
- Excess domestic supply + rising competition limiting pricing power  
- E-auction premium expected stable at ~35–40% (vs ~37% avg in 9MFY26)  
- Prices likely range-bound despite higher global coal prices  

**VOLUME OUTLOOK**  
- No meaningful volume growth visibility in FY26  
- ~4% volume CAGR (FY26–28E) at risk  
- Increasing supply from captive miners impacting growth  

**MARGIN & COST PRESSURES**  
- Upcoming wage revision (July 2026) → cost escalation  
- Limited ability to pass on higher costs  
- Earnings growth expected to remain muted  

**EARNINGS OUTLOOK**  
- EBITDA CAGR: ~4% (FY26–28E)  
- Reflects subdued growth and margin pressures

- Positive for RIL

- Liquidity buffers & LCRs declining across banks  
- Deposit mobilisation lagging credit growth  
- Government spending & FX inflows remain weak  

**MARGIN OUTLOOK**  
- Funding costs rising despite policy easing

- Profitability may face near-term pressure

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Strong credit growth is a positive, but sustainability depends on deposit growth

- Margin compression risk persists due to rising cost of funds  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral (Growth strong, but margins under pressure)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Banking sector is in a high-growth but tightening liquidity phase, where deposit mobilisation and cost of funds will be the key drivers for earnings going forward

**PAINT SECTOR: MORGAN STANLEY VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Demand remains weak, especially in retail segment  
- Price hikes expected across players to support margins  
- Competitive intensity rising with higher discounts and incentives

- Asian Paints: No price hike until Mar 20; hike expected in early April  
- Berger Paints: Already implemented ~4–5% hikes in select solvent-based products (from Mar 21)  
- Industry: Gradual price hikes likely rather than sharp increases

- Increasing aggression from JSW Paints & Akzo Nobel vs earlier periods  
- Higher dealer & painter incentives impacting margins  
- Asian Paints intensifying efforts to regain retail share (especially West India)

- Weak demand + rising competition = pressure on volume growth  
- Price hikes may support margins but risk further demand slowdown  
- Elevated discounting suggests stress in maintaining market share  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral to Slightly Negative  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Paint sector faces a tough near-term setup with weak demand and rising competition, where margin recovery depends on successful price hikes without hurting volumes

**CHEMICAL SECTOR: JEFFERIES VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Middle East conflict disrupting chemical feedstock supply chains  
- India heavily dependent on the region for key raw materials  
- Near-term inventory gains, but margin pressure ahead  

**SECTORAL IMPACT**  
- Most Impacted: Fertilizers  
- Other Affected: Plastics, Paints, Adhesives, Explosives, Consumer Durables  
- Refgas: Sharp input cost inflation → margin pressure  
- Crop Protection: Better positioned due to cost pass-through in export contracts

- Q4 (March quarter): Likely inventory gains due to rising prices  
- From April onwards:  
  • Operating rate cuts possible  
  • Margin compression if supply disruptions persist  
- Companies with pricing power or export-linked contracts relatively safer  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Negative (especially from Q1 FY27 onwards if disruption sustains)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Short-term gains may mask underlying stress, but prolonged conflict can trigger margin compression and production cuts across the chemical value chain

**ASK AUTOMOTIVE: CLSA MAINTAINS OUTPERFORM**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Outperform maintained  
- Target Price: ₹640  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Honda (HMSI) expanding capacity ~28% (6.25mn → 8mn units)  
- ₹1,500 Cr capex at Tapukara plant  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- ASK derives ~35% revenue from HMSI → strong beneficiary  
- Expansion supports higher component demand  
- EV transition tailwind: 30–50% higher content per vehicle vs ICE  

**OUTLOOK**  
- HMSI product push strengthens long-term growth visibility  
- ASK well positioned for next growth phase driven by EV + volume expansion  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- HMSI capacity expansion and EV shift create a strong structural growth tailwind for ASK Automotive

**ALLIED BLENDERS: NUVAMA MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price: ₹670  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Q3 weakness due to regulatory factors (Telangana destocking, Maharashtra policy)  
- Q4 expected to see recovery  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- Premiumisation trend continues; ~50% mix by FY28 (vs 47% currently)  
- ABD Maestro (JV with Ranveer Singh) gaining traction with new launches  
- Focus on luxury positioning through premium portfolio  

**MARGINS & OUTLOOK**  
- EBITDA margin expected to expand to ~17–18% by FY28 (vs 13.2%)  
- Supported by favourable raw material basket, India-UK FTA & backward integration  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong premiumisation strategy and margin expansion visibility support long-term growth despite near-term regulatory disruptions

**KALYAN JEWELLERS: MOSL MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price: ₹550  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Strong demand in India despite rising gold prices  
- Near-term impact expected on MENA business due to geopolitical issues  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- Continued aggressive store expansion in India  
- Candere (online segment) turns profitable with strong momentum  

**MARGINS & FINANCIALS**  
- EBITDA margin expansion driven by better mix & operating leverage  
- Deleveraging and lower finance costs to support profitability  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong domestic demand and improving profitability drivers outweigh near-term geopolitical risks

**STAR HEALTH: JEFFERIES MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price: ₹660  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Loss ratio expected to improve (~200 bps over FY25–28E)  
- Driven by resumed price hikes & selective underwriting  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- Additional levers: claim automation, hospital partnerships, Public Insurance Registry  
- Strong earnings visibility with ~21% IFRS EPS CAGR (FY26–28E)  

**VALUATION**  
- Trading at ~18x FY27E P/E; does not fully reflect growth potential  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Improving profitability metrics and strong growth outlook make current valuations attractive

**PREMIER ENERGIES: DAM CAPITAL MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price: ₹1,295  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Strong competitive moat driven by technology & execution capabilities  
- Superior positioning in solar cell manufacturing  

**COMPETITIVE ADVANTAGE**  
- Best-in-class cell efficiency  
- Ability to commission & stabilise plants without tech tie-ups  
- Faster ramp-up (4–6 months vs 12–18 months for peers)  
- Strong in-house R&D (G12R cells, zero busbar cells)  

**OUTLOOK**  
- Technological edge and execution strength support long-term growth  
- Difficult for peers to replicate capabilities  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong execution + innovation-led moat positions Premier Energies as a key beneficiary in solar manufacturing growth

reports.text:
**HDFC BANK: HSBC MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price cut to ₹990 (from ₹1,070)  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Chairman resignation seen as difference of opinion, not governance concern  
- However, may lead to valuation multiple compression  

**VALUATION VIEW**  
- Core bank valued at ~2.2x FY27E BVPS (vs 2.4x earlier)  

**OUTLOOK**  
- Improvement in operating performance required to offset sentiment impact  
- Fundamentals remain stable  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Leadership change impacts near-term valuations, but long-term story intact subject to performance delivery

**HSBC: FINANCIALS SECTOR VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Concerns over sustained high oil prices impacting growth, inflation & rates  
- Potential pressure on asset quality if macro stress persists  

**KEY DEBATES**  
- PSU vs Private banks positioning  
- Risk of asset quality deterioration  
- Strategy: Position for rate cuts vs prolonged high-rate environment  

**PREFERRED PICKS**  
- Axis Bank  
- ICICI Bank  
- SBI  
- Cholamandalam Finance  
- Shriram Finance  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Mixed  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Macro uncertainty driving sector divergence; selective stock picking preferred amid rate and asset quality concerns

**MORGAN STANLEY: MFIs (CREDIT GUARANTEE SCHEME)**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Govt launches Credit Guarantee Scheme (effective 20 Mar 2026)  
- Covers loans by banks/FIs to NBFC-MFIs for onward lending  
- Scheme valid till 30 June 2026 or ₹2 lakh Cr guarantees (whichever earlier)  

**SECTOR IMPACT**  
- Positive for MFIs amid asset quality recovery phase  
- Supports loan book rebuild and improves credit flow  
- Enhances lender confidence via risk sharing  

**KEY UNCERTAINTIES**  
- Whether diversified NBFCs qualify under MFI definition  
- Clarity on lending rate cap (EBLR/MCLR + spread)  
- ₹3,000 Cr cap applicability: per lender vs aggregate  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong policy support for MFI sector recovery, though finer details will determine actual transmission and benefit extent

**GRASIM: MORGAN STANLEY MAINTAINS OVERWEIGHT**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Overweight maintained  
- Target Price: ₹3,865  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Paints business targeting ₹10,000 Cr revenue within 3 years of full launch (by 2028)  
- Strong execution with supply-side build-out on track  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- B2B e-commerce: Asset-light platform improving procurement efficiency  
- Cellulose: Focus on high-margin products (Lyocell); ~110kt expansion underway  
- Chemicals: Shift from commodity caustic to specialty derivatives & epoxy  

**OUTLOOK**  
- Multiple growth engines across paints, chemicals & digital platforms  
- Focus on value-added segments to drive margins  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong execution visibility and diversified growth drivers position Grasim for long-term value creation

**ITC: MORGAN STANLEY MAINTAINS EQUAL-WEIGHT**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Equal-Weight maintained  
- Target Price: ₹346  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Sharp tax hikes create uncertainty for cigarette business  
- Stock down ~25% YTD (vs ~10% decline in Sensex)  

**KEY MONITORABLES**  
- Pricing actions to offset tax increases  
- Impact on volumes and EBIT growth in Q1FY27  

**OUTLOOK**  
- Near-term pressure due to taxation  
- Recovery dependent on pricing power & demand resilience  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Tax-led uncertainty weighs on sentiment; pricing strategy and volume response remain key for earnings trajectory

**MAX HEALTHCARE: HSBC UPGRADES TO BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Upgrade to Buy  
- Target Price raised to ₹1,125 (from ₹1,060)  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Strong earnings visibility with ~28.5% EPS CAGR (FY26–28E)  
- Growth drivers outweigh near-term issues (doctor exits, MVT disruptions)  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- ~2,240 bed addition (~43% capacity expansion) by FY28E  
- ~60% expansion via brownfield (better capital efficiency & margins)  
- Scaling of large hospitals (Noida & Dwarka)  
- Strong patient inflow via CGHS aiding faster occupancy ramp-up  

**OUTLOOK**  
- Economies of scale to drive margin expansion  
- Robust execution supports long-term growth  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong capacity expansion and execution visibility position Max Healthcare for sustained earnings growth despite near-term challenges

**COFORGE: UBS INITIATES NEUTRAL**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Initiate: Neutral  
- Target Price: ₹1,240  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Strong execution-led growth; ~13–15% organic growth (FY24–25)  
- Estimated ~15% revenue CAGR over FY26–28E  
- Stock corrected ~35% YTD; PEG ~0.8x looks attractive  

**CONCERNS**  
- Acquisition-led growth raises dilution & integration risks  
- Relatively weaker positioning in GenAI (per VECTOR framework)  

**OUTLOOK**  
- Growth visibility remains healthy  
- Risk-reward now balanced at current levels  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong growth track record but concerns on strategy & GenAI positioning limit upside in near term

**COFORGE: MOSL MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price: ₹1,880  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Stock declined ~9–10% post US–Iran conflict (~45% from peak)  
- Underperformed mid-cap peers by ~2–9%  

**CONCERNS**  
- Higher exposure to travel vertical vs peers  
- Exposure to Middle East adds near-term risk  

**VALUATION & OUTLOOK**  
- Current pricing reflects extreme bear-case scenario  
- Attractive valuations at ~19x/15x FY27/28E P/E  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Near-term headwinds priced in; attractive valuations offer upside if risks stabilise

**CG POWER: UBS MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price raised to ₹900 (from ₹840)  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Business at inflection point; worst of industrial weakness largely behind  
- Improvement expected in orders, margins & rail execution  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- Strong positioning in power segment with timely capacity expansion  
- Improving export visibility  
- Strong cash position (post-QIP) supports portfolio expansion  
- OSAT opportunity scalable over long term  

**OUTLOOK**  
- Domestic cycle (rail & industrial motors) turning positive over next 12 months  
- Export scale-up may remain gradual  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Recovery in core segments and strong balance sheet position CG Power for sustained growth ahead

**JEFFERIES: PAYMENT PLATFORMS VIEW**  

**RATING & TARGETS**  
- Paytm: Buy (TP ↓ to ₹1,350 from ₹1,400)  
- Pine Labs: Buy (TP ↓ to ₹260 from ₹300)  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Targeting ~20%+ revenue growth with EBITDA margin expansion  
- Operating leverage to support profitability improvement  
- Expansion in network, loan origination & new business areas  

**SECTOR DYNAMICS**  
- Stocks down ~20–30% YTD due to risk aversion & new listings  
- Higher sensitivity to contribution margins (9–6% impact on adj. EBITDA)  

**OUTLOOK**  
- Strong growth visibility despite near-term sentiment pressure  
- Execution on lending & ecosystem expansion remains key  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Structural growth intact with improving profitability, while current correction offers opportunity despite margin sensitivity risks
